memories.title: CUDA RMSNorm block-parallel row reduction
memories.summary: For hidden-size RMSNorm kernels, map one row to a block and keep the reduction/writeback path row-local.
memories.detail_description: Use a block-level row reduction for RMSNorm instead of scalar or poorly partitioned reductions. Keep the normalization formula and epsilon semantics explicit, then benchmark the same hidden size before and after.
memories.applicability: Applies to row-wise normalization where each row can be reduced independently and hidden size is large enough to amortize block scheduling.
memories.caveats: Do not transfer the RMSNorm formula to Softmax or LayerNorm without rechecking the operation semantics.
operation_semantics: RMSNorm is a row-wise normalization reduction
safe_transfer_notes: Reuse the block-level row reduction structure and coalesced writeback pattern.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not reuse the RMSNorm final formula for Softmax, LayerNorm, or attention.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from EvoInfer operator-campaign verifier fixtures with benchmark and correctness artifacts.

memories.title: CUDA row-wise Softmax shared-memory reduction
memories.summary: For row-wise Softmax, shared-memory max/sum reductions can reduce latency from 0.42 ms to 0.08 ms on the seeded workload.
memories.detail_description: Use row-wise max and sum reductions in shared memory, preserve probability normalization, and verify row sums and max absolute output error after optimization.
memories.applicability: Most useful for row-wise Softmax kernels where the row length fits the block/shared-memory strategy.
memories.caveats: Masked Softmax and attention Softmax require additional mask and numerical-stability checks.
safe_transfer_notes: Reuse the row-wise max/sum reduction pattern after matching row length and dtype.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not transfer to masked Softmax without masked-output correctness evidence.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from EvoInfer operator-campaign verifier fixtures with benchmark, correctness, and profiler evidence.

memories.title: Use FlashInfer as attention baseline before custom kernels
memories.summary: FlashInfer decode/prefill attention baselines exposed large gaps versus naive PyTorch SDPA on RTX 3090 class measurements.
memories.detail_description: Before hand-writing attention kernels, benchmark FlashInfer decode and prefill kernels against the local PyTorch/naive baseline. Use the library result as a reality check and as a source of workload-specific route hints.
memories.applicability: Applies when optimizing LLM attention, KV-cache decode, or prefill paths and FlashInfer supports the target GPU/runtime.
memories.caveats: FlashInfer speedups do not prove custom kernel correctness. Always compare outputs and match sequence length, head layout, dtype, and mask semantics.
operation_semantics: LLM attention with decode and prefill modes
correctness_invariants: Compare attention outputs or downstream logits on the same layout and dtype.
safe_transfer_notes: Use FlashInfer benchmark numbers to decide whether custom kernel work is worth pursuing.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not claim a custom kernel improvement unless it beats the library baseline on the same workload.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from EvoInfer FlashInfer learning report and verifier-backed benchmark table.

memories.title: FLA route-policy memory reduces local search
memories.summary: For FLA-style learning campaigns, route-policy memory reduced average task time from 141.658 s to 99.221 s by avoiding broad local search.
memories.detail_description: When a local search stalls, switch from free exploration to a route-policy artifact: state which backend/library route is being tried, which dtype/workload branches are supported by evidence, and which branches are intentionally skipped. This helped the agent spend less wall-clock time on ungrounded branches.
memories.applicability: Applies to learning campaigns over algorithm libraries such as FLA where the task is to learn reusable route decisions rather than tune a single kernel blindly.
memories.caveats: Route policy is a planning constraint, not proof. It must point to benchmark, correctness, profiler, or source evidence before writing durable memory.
operation_semantics: route selection for inference optimization campaigns
correctness_invariants: Do not skip a candidate branch unless the route decision cites evidence.
safe_transfer_notes: Use compact route-decision artifacts to guide search before editing kernels.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not skip dtype or backend branches just because a prior memory exists; require matching workload evidence.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from EvoInfer memory-vs-no-memory campaign analysis.

memories.title: FLA parallel attention needs SDPA boundary check
memories.summary: On limx RTX 3090, FLA parallel_attn beat the FLA naive reference on long causal attention but was slower than PyTorch SDPA on the tested standard causal workloads.
memories.detail_description: Treat FLA parallel_attn as library-learning evidence, not as a universal replacement for PyTorch SDPA. The verified limx benchmark showed correctness passed for four causal-attention shapes, but FLA parallel_attn was slower than torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention for those standard shapes while still much faster than FLA naive on long sequence. Use this memory to force a baseline boundary check before transferring FLA routes.
memories.applicability: Applies when an agent considers FLA parallel attention for standard causal attention on RTX 3090-class hardware and similar torch/triton/fla versions.
memories.caveats: This does not rule out FLA for specialized recurrent/linear attention workloads. Re-benchmark when dtype, layout, mask semantics, backend version, or GPU changes.
memories.failure_reason: FLA parallel_attn did not beat PyTorch SDPA on the tested standard causal-attention shapes.
correctness_invariants: Compare output tensors against PyTorch SDPA for each B/T/H/D shape.
safe_transfer_notes: Use FLA naive-vs-parallel speedup as evidence that library kernels can teach implementation routes.
Use PyTorch SDPA as a required baseline before claiming improvement.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not present FLA parallel_attn as a win for standard causal attention unless it beats SDPA on the same workload.
Do not transfer this result to retention/GLA/delta-rule kernels without separate artifacts.
memories.rejection_reason: Do not apply as a direct optimization over PyTorch SDPA for matching workloads.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from limx FLA attention benchmark artifacts verified by evoinfer_library_campaign_verifier.

memories.title: Do not skip dtype branches without evidence
memories.summary: A route policy that skips dtype/backend branches without benchmark or source evidence is a negative transfer risk.
memories.detail_description: In route-policy campaigns, the agent may try to use a memory as permission to skip unexplored dtype branches. This is unsafe unless the route decision records matching workload evidence, source constraints, or a failed benchmark for the skipped branch.
memories.applicability: Use as a negative constraint whenever a retrieved memory suggests pruning candidate branches.
memories.caveats: A negative route constraint should not block experimentation when the workload, dtype, or backend is different.
operation_semantics: route selection for inference optimization campaigns
correctness_invariants: Every skipped branch needs a route_decision evidence reference.
safe_transfer_notes: Ask the agent to produce route_decision.json with evidence IDs before pruning.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not treat route memories as authority for mismatched dtype, backend, or workload.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded as a negative transfer constraint from route-policy verifier failures.

memories.title: vLLM/SGLang/TensorRT backend installs can stall on large wheel downloads
memories.summary: On limx, vLLM, SGLang, and TensorRT setup attempts stalled during uv or wheel downloads; treat this as an environment blocker and switch to prebuilt wheels, cache, or containerized installs before benchmarking.
memories.environment: limx RTX 3090, Ubuntu Linux, Python 3.12, NVIDIA driver 580.126.18
memories.issue_signature: pip uv vllm sglang tensorrt download stalls with no log progress
memories.symptoms: Install logs stayed at large downloads such as uv-0.11.21 or TensorRT/ONNX wheels for more than one minute without progress, preventing backend attention benchmarks.
memories.root_cause: The blocker was package acquisition/network throughput rather than an inference-kernel failure. The benchmark should not be recorded as a backend optimization failure.
memories.solution: Before benchmarking these backends, pre-stage wheels or use a pinned container/venv with vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT, ONNX, CUDA, and PyTorch already matched. If online install is required, use a reliable mirror/cache and capture install logs as environment artifacts.
memories.verification: The stalled installer processes were terminated, their logs were saved, and the environment-debug artifact bundle passed EvoInfer library-campaign verification as an environment_debug candidate.
commands: pkill -f 'pip install vllm|pip install sglang|pip install tensorrt'
error_messages: Downloading TensorRT/ONNX wheels with no progress
diagnostic_steps: Started backend installs in separate logs.
Observed no file-size or log progress after repeated checks.
Classified the result as environment debug, not benchmark data.
Wrote environment.json, api_inventory.json, install logs, verifier_result.json, and dream_write_candidates.json.
memories.prevention: Run backend learning campaigns from prebuilt containers or cached wheels; keep install blockers out of optimization benchmark comparisons.
memories.risk: Do not infer vLLM, SGLang, or TensorRT performance from this memory. It only describes setup fragility.
memories.promotion_reason: Seeded from limx backend install debug artifacts verified as an environment_debug campaign.

memories.title: FlashInfer JIT consumed binary TSZ header
memories.summary: A TSZ# binary header in FlashInfer JIT resources indicates the loader consumed a binary artifact as source/config text.
memories.environment: RTX 3090 class GPU with CUDA FlashInfer stack
memories.issue_signature: FlashInfer JIT consumed binary TSZ header
memories.symptoms: FlashInfer import or JIT setup fails, and logs contain TSZ# binary header tokens.
memories.root_cause: The JIT/resource path selected an incompatible or corrupted binary artifact instead of the expected CUDA source/config resource.
memories.solution: Reinstall a CUDA-matched FlashInfer wheel, clear stale build/cache artifacts, then rerun import and smoke benchmarks.
memories.verification: FlashInfer import, show-config, and a smoke benchmark pass after reinstall.
diagnostic_steps: Check FlashInfer import path and package version.
Inspect the JIT log for binary header tokens.
Confirm local CUDA runtime matches the installed wheel.
memories.prevention: Pin FlashInfer wheels by CUDA/PyTorch compatibility and record the install command in environment artifacts.
memories.risk: Blind reinstall can hide a real source-path issue; keep diagnostic logs before changing packages.

memories.title: Voice STT should fall back to CPU when libcuda.so.12 is unavailable
memories.summary: If voice input fails with libcuda.so.12 missing, configure the speech model for CPU instead of loading CUDA.
memories.environment: CPU-only or CUDA runtime unavailable browser/GUI backend
memories.hardware: CPU or GPU host without visible libcuda.so.12
memories.issue_signature: Voice input failed Library libcuda.so.12 is not found or cannot be loaded
memories.symptoms: GUI voice input starts, then fails with libcuda.so.12 not found or cannot be loaded.
memories.root_cause: The speech model selected a CUDA provider even though the runtime cannot load the CUDA driver library.
memories.solution: Force the STT model/device/provider to CPU and avoid importing CUDA-only providers for voice input.
memories.verification: Voice input transcription runs on CPU without attempting to load libcuda.so.12.
error_messages: Library libcuda.so.12 is not found or cannot be loaded
diagnostic_steps: Check whether libcuda.so.12 is visible to the process.
Check the STT provider/device selected at startup.
Rerun transcription with CPU provider forced.
memories.prevention: Default speech input to CPU unless the GUI backend has verified CUDA availability.
memories.risk: CPU transcription is slower, but it is more portable and avoids startup failure.

memories.title: vLLM paged attention decode wins on tested RTX 3090 shapes
memories.summary: On limx RTX 3090, vLLM PagedAttention decode beat an explicit torch softmax reference by 1.85x-7.94x on three fp16 decode workloads.
memories.detail_description: Use vLLM PagedAttention for decode workloads with paged KV cache when the workload matches the tested decode shapes. The experiment wrote KV cache through vLLM cache APIs, then compared decode output against a shape-matched torch softmax reference.
memories.applicability: Applies to fp16 decode attention with paged KV cache on RTX 3090-class GPUs and similar vLLM/torch/CUDA versions.
memories.caveats: Re-check block size, KV head layout, mask semantics, dtype, and vLLM version before transferring.
safe_transfer_notes: Try vLLM paged attention before writing custom decode kernels on matching workloads.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not transfer to prefill attention, sliding-window masks, different KV head grouping, or fp8 without new artifacts.
memories.promotion_reason: Verified by limx backend-attention benchmark with correctness and timing artifacts.

memories.title: SGLang flash attention decode wins on tested RTX 3090 shapes
memories.summary: On limx RTX 3090, sgl_kernel flash_attn_with_kvcache beat an explicit torch softmax reference by 1.62x-4.13x on three fp16 decode workloads.
memories.environment: limx RTX 3090, SGLang 0.5.13.post1, sgl_kernel importable, torch 2.11.0+cu130
memories.detail_description: Use SGLang sgl_kernel flash_attn_with_kvcache as a decode-attention route on matching fp16 KV-cache workloads. Full sglang import can fail from torch/torchvision CUDA-major mismatch, but the kernel package itself can still import and run.
memories.applicability: Applies to fp16 decode attention with contiguous KV cache when sgl_kernel is available and FA3 support check passes.
memories.caveats: Probe sgl_kernel separately before rejecting the route because full SGLang import may fail for unrelated dependency reasons.
correctness_invariants: cache_seqlens and layout must match the kernel docstring
safe_transfer_notes: Try sgl_kernel flash_attn_with_kvcache for decode attention before custom kernels on matching workloads.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not reject sgl_kernel solely because full sglang import fails; inspect the kernel package separately.
memories.promotion_reason: Verified by limx backend-attention benchmark with correctness and timing artifacts.

memories.title: TensorRT attention subgraph has mixed transfer on RTX 3090
memories.summary: TensorRT 11 fixed-shape MatMul-Softmax-MatMul attention passed correctness, but batch=1 seq=2048 was slower than the torch reference.
memories.environment: limx RTX 3090, TensorRT 11.1.0.106, torch 2.8.0+cu126 used for CUDA buffers
memories.detail_description: TensorRT can compile and execute a fixed-shape fp16 attention subgraph on RTX 3090, but it is not a universal win. The tested batch=1 seq=2048 case was slower than torch, while batch=1 seq=512 and batch=4 seq=2048 were faster.
memories.applicability: Use as a route-policy memory when evaluating TensorRT for fixed-shape attention subgraphs.
memories.caveats: Do not transfer to dynamic-shape engines, fused plugins, full model engines, or different streams without fresh benchmark.
correctness_invariants: record positive and negative transfer cases separately
safe_transfer_notes: Try TensorRT only after matching batch/seq/head shape and deployment constraints.
unsafe_transfer_notes: Do not assume TensorRT attention is faster for batch=1 seq=2048 on RTX 3090; this tested shape was a negative transfer.
memories.promotion_reason: Verified by limx TensorRT attention subgraph benchmark, including a recorded negative-transfer case.

memories.title: TensorRT pip install needs NVIDIA PyPI index for binary libs
memories.summary: Installing tensorrt-cu12 from a normal mirror can stall at source metadata/libs; adding https://pypi.nvidia.com resolved the TensorRT runtime wheel download.
memories.issue_signature: Collecting tensorrt_cu12_libs stalls or is extremely slow without NVIDIA package index
memories.symptoms: pip install tensorrt/tensorrt-cu12 stayed in metadata/download stage when only a standard PyPI mirror and PyTorch wheel index were configured.
memories.root_cause: TensorRT runtime libraries are distributed via NVIDIA's Python package index; the normal mirror path may only expose source shim packages or slow binary resolution.
memories.solution: Install with a normal mirror plus --extra-index-url https://pypi.nvidia.com, then verify import of tensorrt, onnx, and numpy.
memories.verification: The command installed tensorrt-cu12 11.1.0.106, tensorrt_cu12_libs 11.1.0.106, tensorrt_cu12_bindings 11.1.0.106 and import probing returned true for tensorrt/onnx/numpy.
memories.risk: The runtime wheel is about 4.3GB; check disk and network before starting. Match CUDA/TensorRT version to the target deployment stack.
commands: python -m pip install tensorrt-cu12 onnx numpy -i https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple --extra-index-url https://pypi.nvidia.com
error_messages: Preparing metadata for tensorrt_cu12_libs stalled without NVIDIA index
diagnostic_steps: Tried standard mirror plus PyTorch cu126 index.
Restarted install with NVIDIA PyPI extra index.
memories.prevention: For TensorRT Python installs, include NVIDIA PyPI index from the first attempt and record wheel size in setup plans.
memories.resolved_by_command: python -m pip install tensorrt-cu12 onnx numpy -i https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple --extra-index-url https://pypi.nvidia.com
memories.promotion_reason: Verified by successful TensorRT install and import probe on limx.
